description: Pure byte-size helpers for pixel-backed resources. It estimates 2D texture/storage sizes from dimensions, pixel format, bytes per pixel, and optional mip levels.

library_notes.general: Single responsibility - one widget does one thing. No global state. No project-specific code, hardcoded paths, credentials, or environment-specific values. Expose variation points as constructor parameters or function arguments - no hardcoded thresholds, colors, sizes, or behavior flags. URLs and endpoints belong in examples as demonstration data, not in src/ - if a widget needs a URL to operate, it must be a parameter the caller provides. All dependencies must be declared in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies. Widgets cannot depend on other widgets - they must be fully self-contained. If you need logic from another widget, copy it into src/ directly. Examples must run and exit cleanly with no user input, no network calls, and no external services or API keys - use fake/hardcoded data. The widget's own declared dependencies are fine and will be installed by the validator before the example runs. Do not modify library_notes - it is managed by Cartograph and will be restored on checkin.
library_notes.language: No project-specific names, identifiers, or terminology anywhere in src/, tests/, or examples/ - use generic names like 'item', 'value', 'exampleUser'. Use std/unittest for all tests (import std/unittest, not bare import unittest). Use func for pure procedures with no side effects — only use proc when side effects are required. Mark all public symbols with * (e.g. func myFunc*(x: int): int). Use camelCase for funcs/procs/variables, PascalCase for types. Prefer stdlib imports with the std/ prefix (e.g. import std/strutils, not import strutils). No echo in src/ files. No quit() or system.quit() in src/ files. No {.importc.} or {.compile.} pragmas — C FFI makes widgets platform-dependent and unvalidatable. No {.global.} pragma — widgets must not use global mutable state. No OS-targeting when defined(windows/linux/posix/etc.) — widgets must validate on all platforms. No cast[seq[T]] in src/ — GC-managed seq casts break GC invariants and are blocked; copy explicitly or use toOpenArrayByte. Avoid bare `except:` — catches Defect and KeyboardInterrupt; use a typed except like `except CatchableError:`. Avoid raw memory primitives (alloc/dealloc/copyMem, cast[ptr ...], ptr UncheckedArray) — prefer GC-managed seq/string; warns if used. A widget_id.nimble file is required at the widget root - it is created by cartograph create and must stay in sync with widget.json dependencies. List nimble package dependencies in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies as 'pkgname >= version'.
library_notes.domain: Transformation logic must be pure and stateless - same input always produces same output, no side effects. Accept the broadest practical input type (bytes, str, file-like, path) and let the caller choose. Expose encoding, delimiter, schema, and other format details as parameters. Raise descriptive errors on malformed input rather than silently coercing or dropping data.
